name: Paul Winter
abstract: Paul Winter (born August 31, 1939) is an American saxophonist, composer, and bandleader. He is considered to be a pioneer of world music, which interweaves the voices of the wild with instrumental voices from classical, jazz, and world music.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Memories of the Future 40 Years Later - An Evening with Oregon and the Paul Winter Consort

about_quotes.speaker: Denny Zeitlin
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Winter, who I had known from back in Chicago and had heard me play and liked my playing... he wanted to get together... I just sort of slipped into recording in 1963... if Paul Winter hadn’t grabbed me by the ear and dragged me into his office.
about_quotes.doc_title: Denny Zeitlin interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: in ’62 I was with Paul Winter, he had a sextet at that time... And we won the Intercollegiate Jazz Festival... Kennedy asked us to do a six month tour of South and Central America, and all the islands, with Paul’s sextet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: he came to me and asked me to arrange an album for him... So, I did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bernie Kirsh
about_quotes.evidence: It was an 8 track recording, I think it was Paul Winter I think was the artist actually. And I was assisting on the date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernie Kirsh interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: He [Warren Bernhardt] did well and worked with Paul Winter; good friend of Bill Evans, really close with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: It was Paul Winter and Miles. We played . . . We played at 8 o’clock. Miles came on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
